metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/reducer/_cache.py` to Scope and add an AC (or amend AC6) requiring `_REDUCER_CACHE_VERSION` to be bumped to v4 with a changelog entry explaining the new `attestations`/`last_reopened_at` projections.
findings.finding: The plan adds two new projected state fields (`attestations` and `last_reopened_at`) that will be absent in all existing `.cache.json` files compiled by the current reducer. The cache invalidation mechanism is `_REDUCER_CACHE_VERSION` in `src/rebar/reducer/_cache.py` (currently at v3, with a documented bump policy: 'BUMP THIS whenever a processor's projection changes'). The plan's Scope lists `_processors.py`, `_present.py`, `_state.py`, `ticket_state.schema.json` — but omits `_cache.py` and never mentions bumping `_REDUCER_CACHE_VERSION`. This is an ambiguous counter increment placement: the policy is clear in the codebase but the plan does not invoke it.
findings.suggested_fix: Bump `_REDUCER_CACHE_VERSION` to 4 and add the comment: 'v4: process_signature now also projects attestations{}; process_status projects last_reopened_at'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add test coverage (unit or integration) for: (a) kind derivation happy-path and unkindable/blank/no-colon edge cases; (b) blank/retired event skipping the attestations map without clobbering an existing kind entry; (c) data['kind'] hint mismatch being ignored; (d) old-snapshot fold-in with valid, blank, and unkindable legacy records; (e) last_reopened_at null baseline and correct timestamp on closed→open transition; (f) public_state() HMAC stripping for the attestations branch.
findings.finding: The plan introduces multiple new behaviors with non-trivial edge cases (kind derivation from manifest[0], unkindable/blank/retired event skipping, data["kind"] hint mismatch, old-snapshot fold-in with dropped records, last_reopened_at null/transition logic, public_state HMAC stripping per kind) but names zero test files, zero test ACs, and zero testing work in scope. No failure, boundary, or invalid-input paths are validated by any stated test.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `tests/reducer/test_attestations_slice.py` (or equivalent) covering each AC with at least one happy-path and one failure/edge-path case, and reference it explicitly in the Scope section. At minimum, ACs should be restated as testable assertions (e.g., 'verified by test_kind_derivation_unkindable').

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or CLAUDE.md entry documenting the dual mirror+map invariant, the kind-derivation rule, the fold-in migration contract for old snapshots, and the explicit statement that 929e owns the retirement/cleanup path for the attestations map.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural pattern — a kind-keyed attestations map with a fold-in migration for old snapshots and a dual mirror+map maintenance contract — but does not mention capturing this decision in an ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design-doc update.
findings.suggested_fix: Create docs/adr/NNN-attestations-map.md (or a CLAUDE.md section) covering: (1) why dual-write is used instead of replacing the mirror, (2) the authoritative kind-derivation rule (manifest[0] before ':'), (3) old-snapshot fold-in semantics, and (4) the explicit assignment of map retirement to 929e.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly assign ownership of attestations map retirement/cleanup (clearing or tombstoning stale kind entries after pin retirement) to a named slice (e.g., 929e), and add an AC in this slice confirming the deferral is tracked.
findings.finding: The attestations map has no retire/cleanup path in this slice — a kind once written is never removed, even after a pin is retired — and the plan does not explicitly assign ownership of map retirement to a named future slice.
findings.location: ## What — MAP (new, additive) / ## Acceptance Criteria AC6
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC6: 'Map retirement (clearing stale kind entries on pin retire) is explicitly deferred to 929e; this slice adds a TODO/NOTE in process_snapshot and process_signature code comments pointing to the 929e tracking issue.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR (or extend ADR 0002) documenting the dual-write mirror strategy: name it as an expand/contract migration, record the mirror-lifetime contract (mirror is removed only in 929e alongside gate migration), and state the rollback invariant (old code reads only `state['signature']`, so any SNAPSHOT written by 114f-code must carry both keys).
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural pattern — dual-write mirror + kind-keyed `attestations` map + fold-in migration + two-level HMAC stripping in `public_state()` — that crosses the 'novel architecture' bright-line and has security/migration implications, yet no ADR is proposed or cited to document the deliberate design choices (why dual-write instead of a single canonical map with a compatibility shim, what the rollback invariants are, when the mirror contract ends).
findings.location: What section / Scope section — 'per-key LWW, like process_workflow_run'
findings.suggested_fix: Create `docs/adr/0009-attestations-state-model-split.md` covering: (1) why dual-write over a single canonical key with a shim, (2) mirror lifetime (retired in 929e, not before), (3) rollback invariant for SNAPSHOT back-compat, (4) HMAC-strip two-level contract. Reference it from the plan's Scope section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define an authoritative kind vocabulary (e.g., a constants module or named constants co-located with the processor or schema) enumerating at minimum `'plan-review'` and `'completion-verifier'`, and reference them from AC2 and the `ticket_state.schema.json` update.
findings.finding: The `kind` value vocabulary for `attestations[kind]` is referenced throughout but never enumerated as a closed set or defined constants. The plan derives kind from `manifest[0]` prefix (substring before first ':'), but the only kind strings established in the codebase are `_MANIFEST_PREFIX = 'plan-review'` (attest.py:30) and `'completion-verifier'` (transition.py:297). The plan says '114f owns the canonical per-record shape' but the kind-key vocabulary itself is never defined — no enum, no constants module, no exhaustive list. An implementer adding a future attestation type has no authoritative reference for what a valid kind string looks like.
findings.location: What section: 'Kind = SIGNED manifest[0] substring before the first ":" (authoritative)'; AC2: 'Kind from authoritative manifest[0]'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `_ATTESTATION_KINDS` constant (e.g., in `_processors.py` or a new `_attestation.py`) with `PLAN_REVIEW = 'plan-review'` and `COMPLETION_VERIFIER = 'completion-verifier'`, and reference them in `process_signature` kind derivation and in `ticket_state.schema.json` as a pattern/enum for the `attestations` map keys.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out note: state that old-code readers either (a) tolerate unknown top-level keys (cite evidence) so rollback is safe, or (b) describe an expand-contract step (e.g. deploy forward-compatible reader first, then writer, with a named revert procedure).
findings.finding: The plan labels itself 'ADDITIVE and behavior-preserving' and acknowledges existing consumers, but provides no explicit rollback/back-out plan for the state-store schema change. T4 requires an explicit reversibility mechanism, not just acknowledgement of additive intent — specifically: what happens to persisted state carrying `attestations` if the code is rolled back to a version that does not know about this key?
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback safety' paragraph: confirm that all existing state readers use `state.get(...)` / dict access that silently ignores unknown keys, making code rollback safe without a data migration; or, if strict parsing is used, specify a two-phase deploy.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In the What section (fold-in) and AC4, explicitly state the guard condition as 'if `attestations` is absent from `compiled_state` AND `signature` is present and kindable' — matching the managed_refs precedent pattern in `process_snapshot` — so the implementer has a concrete, co-located predicate.
findings.finding: The plan's fold-in rule for old snapshots ('an OLD SNAPSHOT whose compiled_state carries only `signature` (no `attestations`) is folded into the map on restore') is a processing protocol applied inside `process_snapshot`, but the guard condition for distinguishing old vs new SNAPSHOTs is only stated as a prose implication ('no `attestations`'), not as an explicit implementable predicate co-located with the existing `process_snapshot` code. The plan also does not specify the idempotency behavior when a new-format SNAPSHOT already carries both `attestations` AND `signature` — should the fold-in be skipped entirely, and how does that interact with the managed_refs precedent (`if 'managed_refs' not in compiled_state`).
findings.location: What section: 'Read fold-in: an OLD SNAPSHOT whose compiled_state carries only `signature` (no `attestations`) is folded into the map'; src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py:529-543
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC4: 'Guard: fold-in runs iff `"attestations" not in compiled_state` and `compiled_state.get("signature")` is present and kindable (parallel to the managed_refs migration guard at _processors.py:542).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify what constitutes a 'derivable kind' when manifest[0] contains no ':' character — state explicitly whether the whole string is used as the kind or the entry is treated as unkindable.
findings.finding: The plan defines 'kindable' as 'non-empty manifest with a derivable kind' but never specifies what makes a kind 'derivable' — specifically, whether manifest[0] with no ':' character yields a kind (the whole string) or is treated as unkindable. An implementer has two equally plausible interpretations.
findings.location: What section — 'if the event is kindable (non-empty manifest with a derivable kind)' and 'Kind = SIGNED manifest[0] substring before the first ":"'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: 'A manifest[0] value with no ":" is unkindable (the empty prefix is not a valid kind); only entries of the form "<kind>:<rest>" produce a map entry.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the exact predicate for when data["kind"] 'matches' — specify whether it means strict equality with the manifest-derived kind string, and whether a mismatch produces a warning/log or is purely silently discarded.
findings.finding: The plan states 'data["kind"] is used only as a hint iff it matches' but never defines what 'matches' means — equality with the derived manifest kind? Substring? Case-insensitive? An implementer cannot determine the comparison without guessing.
findings.location: What section — 'data["kind"] is used only as a hint iff it matches, else ignored'
findings.suggested_fix: Add: 'A hint "matches" iff data["kind"] == derived_kind (exact string equality); a mismatch is silently discarded with no error or log.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in AC6 (and the What section) exactly where an empty attestations map is omitted — SNAPSHOT payload only, public_state() output only, or both — so the implementing developer applies the rule to the correct file(s).
findings.finding: AC6 states 'empty attestations omitted' without specifying the scope of omission — omitted from the SNAPSHOT payload only, from public_state() output only, or from both. Each requires different implementation in different files.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC6: 'Compaction/SNAPSHOT round-trips all kinds; empty attestations omitted'
findings.suggested_fix: Revise AC6 to read: 'empty attestations map is omitted from both the SNAPSHOT payload (sparse storage) and public_state() output (consistent with other optional state keys).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an acceptance criterion that verifies `ticket_state.schema.json` is updated to include both `attestations` (kind-keyed map) and `last_reopened_at`, and that existing valid state still validates against the updated schema.
findings.finding: The Scope lists `ticket_state.schema.json` and `_state.py` as changed files, but no acceptance criterion verifies the schema update (e.g. that `attestations` and `last_reopened_at` appear in the JSON schema and that the schema validates correctly against new/old state shapes).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a sentence to the What/Scope section explicitly specifying that `attestations` is omitted from the SNAPSHOT payload when the map is empty, consistent with AC6.
findings.finding: AC6 states 'empty `attestations` omitted' during compaction/SNAPSHOT, but the plan body does not describe this behaviour anywhere — the What section only says 'New SNAPSHOTs carry BOTH `attestations` and the `signature` mirror automatically', with no mention of the empty-map omission rule.
findings.location: AC6 / What section (Read fold-in / SNAPSHOT paragraph)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact `data["kind"]` match rule in the plan: define what 'matches' means (e.g., 'equals the kind derived from manifest[0]') and what 'else ignored' means (e.g., 'proceed using manifest[0]-derived kind only, discard data["kind"]'), co-located with or referenced from AC2.
findings.finding: The `data["kind"]` hint validation rule ('`data["kind"]` is used only as a hint iff it matches, else ignored') references a comparison but never defines what it must match against. An implementer cannot determine: (a) whether 'matches' means equality with the manifest-derived kind, (b) whether it means membership in an allowed-kinds set, or (c) whether a type check is also required. This is a processing decision rule co-required with the `process_signature` schema update, but it is specified only as a vague prose clause with no co-located definition.
findings.location: What section: 'data["kind"] is used only as a hint iff it matches, else ignored'; AC2: 'data["kind"] only a validated hint'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2: 'data["kind"] is accepted iff it equals the kind derived from manifest[0]; if they differ, data["kind"] is discarded and manifest[0] is authoritative.'
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Consider splitting `last_reopened_at` into its own sub-task (or explicitly justify its inclusion in this slice beyond 'consumed by 929e'), so the title matches the full body scope and the attestations fix can be merged independently.
findings.finding: `last_reopened_at` (added in `process_status`) is a second independent goal not named in the title 'attestations{} kind-keyed map', causing minor scope drift — the title sets the expectation of a pure attestations-map slice.
findings.suggested_fix: Either update the title to 'Reducer + state model: attestations{} kind-keyed map + last_reopened_at tracking' to reflect actual scope, or extract last_reopened_at to the 929e ticket where it is consumed, keeping this slice purely additive to the attestations model.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Consolidate mirror-preservation coverage into one criterion (e.g. keep AC1's per-event wording and fold `retire_attested_pin` unchanged into AC6's compaction/round-trip criterion, removing the mirror-semantics duplication).
findings.finding: AC1 and AC6 both assert that mirror semantics are unchanged, creating a near-duplicate requirement: AC1 says 'process_signature sets the mirror for EVERY event (old semantics preserved, incl. blank/retired clearing the mirror)' and AC6 says '`retire_attested_pin`/mirror semantics unchanged in this slice'.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
